titlestmt.titleproper: Ben Grauer collection on French political, legal, economic, and social history4636
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Ben Grauer collection on French political, legal, economic, and social history

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Conti, Louis-François-Joseph de Bourbon, prince de, 1734-1814.

did.physdesc: Documents, manuscripts, letters, chapbooks, engravings, photographs, drawings, offprints, and clippings.
did.abstract: Documents, manuscripts, letters, chapbooks, engravings, photographs, drawings, offprints, and clippings providing detailed documentation of French political, legal, economic, and social history from the 16th to early 20th century, especially focussing on local government administration and civil law from ca. 1790-1810.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Documents, manuscripts, letters, chapbooks, engravings, photographs, drawings, offprints, and clippings providing detailed documentation of French political, legal, economic, and social history from the 16th to early 20th century, especially focussing on local government administration and civil law from ca. 1790-1810. Documents include many on the administration of the Département du Nord and the departments of the Gironde and Isère in the 1790's, including certificates of baptism and other documents on questions of residence, and documents on the identification and arrest of emigrés from France. Also included are many legal documents ranging from 1629-1810, including opinions and verdicts in civil lawsuits; documents on issues of inheritance and succession; 18th-century marriage contracts and divorce papers; deeds to houses and land titles; and powers of attorney. Other documents include a military discharge dated 1582, military service and pension records, and other documents concerning the French army from 1710-1815.

Also included are census data for Albania and Dalmatia for 1781; correspondence of various French government officials, from the 1870's and early 20th century; detailed work contracts for repairs and rebuilding of a chateau in 1803, signed by carpenter Jean Baptiste Echeuvin and by Jean Pierre Fluchaire; the autograph text of an address to the king by Monseigneur Leveque on behalf of France's bishops in 1735; a chapbook quoting extensively from Voltaire, especially on the subject of women and on Christianity; a book-length manuscript on the Jesuits, written after 1810; manuscript essays on the relationship of church and state, apparently dating from the 18th century; a genealogy of the family Le Brun de Rabot, dated 1826; letters of recommendation for students seeking admission to a school, by Marc-Antoine Jullien; an essay criticizing the Gazette de France and the French government for not upholding freedom of the press, apparently 18th century; and correspondence by various 19th-century journalists on editing, publishing and social matters.

Photographs, apparently of the 19th century, show views of Venice and Avignon. Engravings include views of Amiens in the early 19th century and portraits of French authors René Charles de Maupeou, Paul Deroulède, Victorien Sardou, Ludovic Halévy, Mme. Eugènie Foa, and Othenin, comte d'Haussonville. Correspondents include Louis Barthou; Henri Rochefort; René-Richard Castel; Barthelemy St. Hilaire; Augustin Louis, marquis de Ximenès; Louis-Joseph de Bourbon, prince of Conti; comte Arthur O'Mahony; Artaud de Montor; Alexis Nicolas Pérignon; Auguste Maguet; Jules Ferry; Miguet, secretary of the Institut impérial de France, later the Institut de France; Jules Michelet; A. Augustin-Louis Laya; Jules Lecomte; Othenin, comte d'Haussonville; Calmann Lévy; Adèle Esquiros; Gabriel, comte d'Haussonville; Etienne Vigée; and Auguste Guinard.

odd.head: NOTES
odd.p: A few documents are on vellum.
Large collections of related books, manuscripts, and graphics from the French Revolutionary and Napoleonic periods are also held in the repository.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Ben Grauer Collection on French political, legal, economic, and social history, #4636. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: France -- History -- Consulate and First Empire, 1799-1815.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: Series I. Ben Grauer Collection on French Political, Legal, Economic, and Social History
AMS. Etienne Arago. Two poems
ALS. Bourgeois, Emile. Letter to Moncher President
ALS. Bourgeois, Emile. To Mon cher President. Versailles
ALS. J. G. Humann ? To Laisne de Villeveque. Paris
ALS. Montalembert. To Ange de St. Priest. Paris
Statutes of L'Oriol. Conserving the land of L'Oriol against foreigners.
AM. Memoire on the disposition of cures dependent on the Abby of St Michel en l'Herme. Lucon,
AMS. M. Marmon tel. Five short stories titled, "La veillee".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 45 pages. "during the troubles of Paris ... in the country

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: D. 69 pages. Copies and extracts of correspondance of Voltaire, 1768. Various verse, essays, translations from many authors and works. n.p., 1768-1821.

did.unittitle: Chapbook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 22 pages. Various copies of verse, songs, quotes, essays. Acrostic S.DEBOURBON. n.p., n.d.

scopecontent.p: Many editorial annotations and corrections in two or three different hands

scopecontent.p: 8 items including one 14 page estimate for repairs of a burned chateau.

did.unittitle: DS. Cabart, Francois Miron and many official signatures. Orleans
Series VII. Property matters in Lot and Garonne
ALS. Labres Delpu?. Algen,
ALS. Malile. Paris
ADS. Malile. Paris
Dept. of Morbihan. 4 ALS to the Mayor of Neant from the Sous Prefet of Ploermel. Du Boishumos; Du Jongand.
Documents from the Dept. du Nord.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 23 items. Copies of birth and death certificates, commune deliberations, mortgages, emigration papers, passports. From Avesnes, Mariembourg, Floyon, Lille, Tournay*, Valenciennes, Beaurepaire, Avesnelles, Feron, Foret, Dourlers, Quesnoy. *1646 document folded twice

did.unittitle: DS. Extrait des Minutes de la Secretairerie d' Etat. Schoenbrunn,
DS. Pailliez. Account sheets for the Bureau de Pavilly, Dept. de la Seine Inferiure, for the trimestre of October 1820. Pavilly
Series VIII. Seine Inferieure
LS. Girand in (Stanislaus, comte de). Rauen
LS. Girandin. Rouen
ALS. Girardin. Ermenonville
DS. Champy, le maire. Extrait du registres des actes de naissances, mariages at sepultures de Dammartin (Seine et Marne
OS. Graimprelle, and others. Riviere, notary publc. Inheritance. Montmorency
DS. A. Maromere ? and others. Judgement of the Civil Court of the Dept./Seine. Paris,
